blocks:
Ayahs

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (۱)
Say, ‘O faithless ones! (1)
لَآ أَعْبُدُ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ (۲)
I do not worship what you worship, (2)
وَلَآ أَنتُمْ عَـٰبِدُونَ مَآ أَعْبُدُ (۳)
nor do you worship what I worship; (3)
وَلَآ أَنَا۠ عَابِدٌ مَّا عَبَدتُّمْ (٤)
nor will I worship what you have worshipped, (4)
وَلَآ أَنتُمْ عَـٰبِدُونَ مَآ أَعْبُدُ (٥)
nor will you worship what I worship. (5)
لَكُمْ دِينُكُمْ وَلِىَ دِينِ (٦)
To you your religion, and to me my religion.’ (6)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of this Surah, it is narrated that some leaders of Quraysh said to the Prophet: "O Muhammad, follow our religion for one year, and we will follow yours the next year. Then, whichever is better, we will continue with it." But the Prophet did not accept. They said: "At least touch our gods and seek blessing from them, so that we may also worship your God." It was at this moment that this Surah was revealed.

The word "Say" (قل) slightly reduces the malice of the disbelievers towards the Prophet, because they realize that their dealings are with Allah, and the Prophet is merely an agent (or messenger).

Sometimes, opponents must be left to their own devices, and one must say: "لكم دینكم" (For you is your religion), just as we read in Surah Al-Qasas, Ayah 55: "لنا اعمالنا و لكم اعمالكم" (For us are our deeds, and for you are your deeds). This is a form of contempt and threat, not a permission for every kind of action or belief.

In some instances in the Quran, repetition is for emphasis, such as: "كلاّ سوف تعلمون ثمّ كلاّ سوف تعلمون"(225) (No! You will soon know, then No! You will soon know) and "فقتل كیف قدّر ثم قتل كیف قدّر"(226) (Then may he be destroyed how he determined, then may he be destroyed how he determined). In this Surah too, the repetition can be for emphasis, so that the polytheists lose hope of the Muslims submitting, and it can also be for instilling steadfastness in the believers so that they remain firm in their positions.

The news that a group of disbelievers will not believe has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran, including Ayah 6 of Surah Al-Baqarah and Ayah 7 of Surah Ya Sin, which explicitly state: "فهم لا یؤمنون" (So they will not believe).

225) Takathur, 3 and 4. 226) Muddaththir, 19 and 20.

Messages

1- The Noble Prophet is the trustee of revelation and conveys whatever is revealed to Him. "Say" (قل).

2- Muslims must respond clearly and decisively to illogical proposals and make their enemies despair of them. "لا اعبد ما تعبدون" (I do not worship what you worship).

3- In the name of unity, one must not violate principles and values. "لا اعبد ما تعبدون" (I do not worship what you worship).

4- The world of disbelief must despair of the Muslims submitting. "لا اعبد... و لا انا عابد" (I do not worship... nor am I a worshipper).

5- The declaration of dissociation (Bara'ah) from the disbelievers must be repeated, emphasized, and public. "قل یا ایّها الكافرون لا اعبد ما تعبدون" (Say, O disbelievers, I do not worship what you worship).

6- Do not compromise in religion, do not appease or flatter the enemy, and in response to the repetition of inappropriate proposals, you must also decisively repeat your position. "لا اعبد... لا انتم عابدون... و لا انا عابد..." (I do not worship... nor are you worshippers... nor am I a worshipper...).

7- Do not resort to every action just to attract others. "لا اعبد... و لا انتم عابدون" (I do not worship... nor are you worshippers).

8- The Quran gives news of the unseen (Ghayb). Not even one of those who made the proposal was willing to embrace Islam. "و لا انتم عابدون ما اعبد" (Nor are you worshippers of what I worship).

9- Steadfastness and decisiveness are conditions for leadership. "و لا انا عابد..." (Nor am I a worshipper...).

10- The worship and servitude of Allah is the criterion distinguishing the believer from the disbeliever. "یا ایّها الكافرون... لا انتم عابدون ما اعبد" (O disbelievers... nor are you worshippers of what I worship).

11- Man is free in choosing religion, not compelled. "لكم دینكم و لى دین" (For you is your religion, and for me is my religion).

12- The ranks must be separated; truth must be distinguished from falsehood, and disbelief from faith. "لكم دینكم ولى دین" (For you is your religion, and for me is my religion).

13- The object of worship (Ma'bud) of every person indicates his religion and creed. "لا انا عابد... لا انتم عابدون... لكم دینكم ولى دین" (Nor am I a worshipper... nor are you worshippers... For you is your religion, and for me is my religion).
